last_run_window: Mon 13 Jul 2026 ~9:30 AM MYT (delta window: 4 new stories + 2 updates; overnight action: Iran escalation continuation oil spike, data-center grid curtailment forecast critical; earnings catalysts: June CPI Jul 14, ASML Jul 15, TSMC Jul 16; next catalysts FOMC Jul 28–29, Section 122 expiry Jul 24)

stories.headline: Samsung Electronics Q2 2026 Earnings Miss Expectations Despite Record Revenue; Memory/Chip Guidance Signals Lower 2H 2026 Demand Growth; Triggers Global Semiconductor Sector Selloff (Nasdaq-100 –1.8%, Intel –9.7%, AMD –6.5%, ASML –7.3%)

US Airstrikes on Iran (Jul 12–13); Oil Surges +3% Overnight to ~$74/bbl; Geopolitical Risk Premium Re-Enters Markets; Rate-Hike Expectations Rise; New Ceasefire Breach Signals Escalation Continues Into Late July

US Data-Center Power Demand Now Acute Constraint: Gartner Forecasts 40% of AI Data Centers Restricted by 2027; AI Server Electricity Demand Surges to 175 TWh (2026) from 95 TWh (2025); 75 Projects Worth $130B Postponed/Canceled; PJM Interconnection Requests Emergency Power Cutover Protocols

Fed Minutes Release July 8, 2:00 PM ET: Nine of 18 Officials Project ≥1 Rate Hike in Remainder 2026; Fed Chair Warsh Omits Own Projection from June Dot Plot, Signals Data Dependency and Potential Hawkish Surprise

May 2026 CPI Prints 4.2% Headline, 2.9% Core; Reverses Three Months of Disinflation, Re-Ignites Rate-Hike Debate Amid June NFP Miss (+57K); June CPI Due Jul 14 08:30 ET (Consensus 3.9% Headline, Core 2.9%)

Section 301 China Tariff Hearing Concludes July 7; 12.5% Rate on Chinese Goods Finalized for July 15 Effective Date; Overlaps with Section 122 (10%) Expiry Jul 24; Dual-Tariff Window Creates Capex Uncertainty; Raises Semiconductor Input Costs and AI Chip Materials Prices
Goldman Sachs Downgrades Semiconductor Sector; Cuts SMH Price Target 5%, Cites 2027 Capex Intensity and 3–5% Earnings Revisions Down; Earnings Reset Now Reflected in Samsung/Global Chip Selloff

ASML Q2 2026 Earnings Released Jul 15; Consensus $7.94 EPS, $10.27B Revenue; EUV Demand for AI Chips Remains Robust; Options Market Pricing 8.36% Post-Earnings Swing; Analysts Bullish (BofA, Bernstein Raise Targets); Stock YTD +65%

TSMC Q2 2026 Earnings Released Jul 16; Consensus $3.81 EPS, $40.04B Revenue (vs. $2.47 / $30.07B YoY); Gross Margin Expected 65.5–67.5%; CoWoS Capacity and AI Pricing Under Scrutiny; Analysts Bullish on AI Demand Path

June NFP +57K (Miss vs. 110K Consensus); Downward Revisions to May/April Payrolls; September Rate-Hike Odds Drop to ~58% (from 67%) Despite Warsh 'Inflation Too High' Stance; Conflicting Fed Signals on Terminal Rate Path
Intel Foundry Services Backlog Grows to $15B+ (from $8B Six Months Prior); Apple Partnership Validates Foundry-as-Service Model; Idled Fab Capacity Filling; Partial Offset to Samsung Capex Doubt

Micron Technology Announces Up to $3 Billion Strategic Investment in US Semiconductor Ecosystem; $500M to GlobalWafers for 300mm Raw Silicon Wafer Manufacturing; 10-Year Supply Agreement; Supports AI Capacity Buildout; Signals Capex Durability Conviction

Section 122 Global Tariff (10%) Expires July 24; Congress Unlikely to Renew Before July 25 Recess; Creates Dual-Tariff Window (12.5% Section 301 + 10% Section 122) Jul 15–24, Then Uncertainty; Drives 2–3 Week Capex Hold for Fabs

US State Department Signals Potential TSMC Export Rule Relaxation if Company Commits to US-Based N-1 (Leading-Edge) Fabrication by 2028; First Explicit Carrot in Negotiated Capacity Buildout; De-Escalation Signal on Export Controls

NVIDIA H200 Yield Issues Emerging at Scale; Advanced Node Design Tool Limitations Approaching; Impacts Near-Term GPU Supply and Pricing Power; Technical Constraints on Production Ramp vs. Demand Growth
